import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import sys
sys.path.insert\(0, '/mnt/c/Users/Paolo/ReorderingSurvey-2026/scripts'\)

# Replicate the script's logic
df_analysis = pd.read_csv\('/mnt/c/Users/Paolo/ReorderingSurvey-2026/results/results_analysis.csv'\)

METRICS = ['rel_bandwidth', 'rel_row_spread', 'locality_vertical_adjacency_ratio', 
           'block_density_8', 'block_density_32', 'block_density_128']

ALL_METRICS = {
    'rel_bandwidth': {'higher_is_better': False},
    'rel_row_spread': {'higher_is_better': False},
    'locality_vertical_adjacency_ratio': {'higher_is_better': True},
    'block_density_8': {'higher_is_better': True},
    'block_density_32': {'higher_is_better': True},
    'block_density_128': {'higher_is_better': True},
}

# Check which metrics exist
metrics = [m for m in METRICS
           if m in df_analysis.columns and ALL_METRICS[m].get\('higher_is_better'\) is not None]
print\(f"Metrics to use: {metrics}"\)
print\(f"Total rows in analysis: {len\(df_analysis\)}"\)

# Try to get originals
originals = df_analysis[df_analysis['perm'] == 'None'][['matrix'] + metrics].copy\(\)
print\(f"Baseline rows found: {len\(originals\)}"\)

if len\(originals\) == 0:
    print\("NO BASELINE ROWS! This is the problem."\)
    # Let's see what happens when we try to merge with empty DataFrame
    reordered = df_analysis[df_analysis['perm'] != 'None'].copy\(\)
    originals_grouped = originals.groupby\('matrix'\)[metrics].mean\(\).reset_index\(\)
    print\(f"Originals grouped shape: {originals_grouped.shape}"\)
    
    result = reordered.merge\(originals_grouped, on='matrix', how='left'\)
    print\(f"After merge, sample columns that should have orig values:"\)
    print\(result[['matrix', 'perm', 'rel_bandwidth']].head\(\)\)
EOF)
